title: Service Outcomes, Boundaries, and Server Lifecycle · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent service team receives a different API, load, trust, persistence, and failure constraint and must transfer service outcomes, boundaries, and server lifecycle into a new service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.
Trace user agent, client, proxy, gateway, origin handler, dependency, and response roles without assigning one component evidence it cannot observe.
Distinguish process, listener, connection, stream, request, handler, goroutine, dependency, and durable-state lifetimes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Service Outcome Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 4202 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then drain the old revision while a canary fails its changed-case invariant. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Service Outcome Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success. For Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Service Outcome Contract, keep Service Outcome Contract connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Lifecycle Evidence Ownership: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 4211 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign startup, request, dependency, shutdown, rollback, alert, and data-recovery evidence to named owners and done conditions.

Evidence target: Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Lifecycle Evidence Ownership.
steps.why: Retrieving Lifecycle Evidence Ownership through the concrete service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Lifecycle Evidence Ownership, keep Lifecycle Evidence Ownership connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Assign startup, request, dependency, shutdown, rollback, alert, and data-recovery evidence to named owners and done conditions.
